description: Clarify that the exponent 2δ−1 = 0.062 (from the BK framework) governs the growth rate of R(d), the maximal number of representations of d, and not directly the density of covered denominators. BK only proves that the density approaches 1 as N → ∞ but does not provide an effective rate for the density convergence. Emphasize that the observed logarithmic fit is empirical and not theoretically derived from BK.
action_taken: Clarify that the exponent 2δ−1 = 0.062 (from the BK framework) governs the growth rate of R(d), the maximal number of representations of d, and not directly the density of covered denominators.
